briefs.title: Short-Term Rental Rules in BC Now Cover First Nation Laws — Hosts Must Comply
briefs.summary: BC updated its Short-Term Rental Accommodations Regulation to include First Nation laws alongside municipal bylaws. This means short-term rental hosts (e.g. Airbnb, VRBO operators) in participating First Nation territories must now follow those communities' rental rules, and platforms must enforce them. The review process for disputed decisions was also updated, shifting authority to a registrar (the official who manages the provincial registry).
briefs.action_required: Check if your short-term rental property falls within a participating First Nation territory and confirm you meet their rental rules.

key_dates.label: BC Short-Term Rental First Nation law requirements take effect
